category_title: Students with autism spectrum disorder who shift conversational topics to personal interests
minimal_context: The student is participating in a collaborative group activity where they must describe the contents of a picture card to their peers.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Jeff, please tell your group what animals you have on your picture card.

STUDENT: I don’t got none here.

TUTOR: Look at the other side of the card.

STUDENT: There is a sheep.

TUTOR: What else do you see?

STUDENT: There are three sheeps and they have little foots.

options.A: Provide frequent reminders and cues to the student during the group activity.
options.B: Ask the student, "Did you say foots or feet?" during the group activity.
options.C: Stay calm and confident when dealing with control issues during the group activity.
options.D: Provide direct reassurance, validate the task's difficulty, and highlight the student's hard work.
